channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Quote of the day by Sam Altman: ;It also takes a lot of energy to train a human' — a staunch defense of the cost of AI training
item.description: The OpenAI CEO equates human intelligence with machine intelligence as he defends the massively expanding energy footprint

item.title: How To Watch the 2026 FIFA World Cup Finals: Spain vs. Argentina
item.description: The end of the FIFA Men’s World Cup is nigh. Here’s how to watch the final games and the first ever World Cup halftime show.

item.title: Elon Musk bought a gas turbine company
item.description: It's likely to become a direct power source for SpaceXAI's data centers.

item.title: xAI sues a man for using Grok to generate CSAM ‘deepfakes’
item.description: The Elon Musk-owned xAI is suing a South Carolina man who allegedly used the company's Grok AI chatbot to generate child sexual abuse material (CSAM). In a lawsuit reported earlier by Reuters, xAI claims Terry Wayne Harwood "knowingly and intentionally used Grok to circumvent safeguards, alter nonconsensual images, and generate and distribute CSAM," breaching the […]...

item.title: Thinking Machines open sources first multimodal language model, Inkling, focused on low cost and 'resistance to censorship'
item.description: Enterprises looking to move more of their agentic AI workloads to open weights models they can customize, control and run on-premises or in virtual private clouds have a strong new contender to consider.Today, Thinking Machines—the highly capitalized American AI startup founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati—released Inkling, its first major language model under an enterprise-friendly Apache 2.0 open source license, and it boasts high, if sub state-of-the-art, performance for open weights models on third-party benchmarks, specifically software engineering (77.6% on SWE-bench Verified, where ...

item.title: Brendan Carr plans to let broadcast giants dominate the airwaves
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission will vote next month on whether a single company can own broadcast stations that reach more than 39 percent of US TV households. In a Breitbart op-ed on Wednesday, Republican Chair Brendan Carr announced an August 6th vote to end the national ownership cap rule, which was intended to prevent one […]

item.title: Orbiting space data centers may face an unexpected hurdle — from environmental politics
item.description: Environmental organizations want federal regulators to pause orbital data center approvals until comprehensive reviews examine cumulative environmental impacts and risks.

item.title: Intel Officials Predict the Pentagon’s Bill for the Iran War Will Exceed $100 Billion
item.description: The Trump administration has not disclosed its cost estimates for the Iran war.

item.title: Workers could get a 14.5% raise if union membership tripled
item.description: A new report from the Economic Policy Institute (EPI) finds that boosting unionization would put an additional $1.2 trillion in the pockets of workers annually. In 2025, the number of American workers represented by a union climbed to 16.5 million, the highest share of unionized workers in 16 years. During an era of declining union membership, this is no small feat. The number of workers who long to be part of a union, however, is far higher—over 50 million....

item.title: Here’s the Truth About Whether Meta’s NameTag Face Recognition Tech ‘Exists’
item.description: Since WIRED reported on Meta’s NameTag face recognition system, company executives have made confusing and conflicting remarks about its very existence.

item.title: GEs viral Opal nugget ice maker now comes in a mini version
item.description: The GE Profile Opal Mini Nugget Ice Maker, Opal Mini Pro, and Opal Mini Pro+ are all available as of July 14. The new ice maker is 30 percent smaller than the Opal Ultra.

item.title: OpenAIs first physical device is here, and its not what we expected
item.description: OpenAI's first hardware product is a $230 mechanical keyboard, not the AI companion device many expected.

item.title: Relax, Apple, OpenAI and its rumored AI smart speaker plans are no threat to you, Siri, HomePods, robots, or any other part of your business
item.description: Fresh rumors point to OpenAI working on a human-like, screenless AI-powered smart speaker. If true, it sounds terrible.

item.title: Taco Bell is removing fresh ingredients from some restaurants. Here’s why
item.description: A nationwide investigation into a foodborne parasite outbreak is examining fresh produce, with Taco Bell taking precautionary steps while the probe continues. With thousands of people sick from a mysterious foodborne illness, your go-to fast food order might look a little different for now.

item.title: OpenAI’s first device could be a screenless smart speaker. It has plenty of competition
item.description: The future of smart speakers could play out not just over hardware, but the LLMs used to power them, too. OpenAI is reportedly developing a screenless smart speaker that could be commercially released as soon as 2027 as its first-ever major hardware device.

item.title: Meta smart glasses get pro cyclist kicked out of race
item.description: Pro cyclist Willie Smit was disqualified from a race in China after wearing Oakley Meta smart glasses to record footage.

item.title: Tesla driver in fatal Texas crash pressed accelerator 100%, NTSB confirms
item.description: The safety board confirmed Tesla's account of the crash, which the company shared days after it happened last month.

item.title: ‘More bad news’ for people struggling with energy bills: AI data centers are driving costs even higher
item.description: PJM, the nation’s largest grid operator, just revealed how demand for electricity is growing faster than supply, thanks in large part to the data center boom. The rapid growth of data centers across the country has already stressed power demand and driven up electricity bills. And those impacts are only worsening.

item.title: Samsung isn't sure why some Galaxy S26 Ultra screens are turning red
item.description: Samsung appears to be internally investigating the discoloration defect.

item.title: European Commission will not force smartwatches and other wearables to have replaceable batteries
item.description: The EU adopted regulation in 2023 that requires many electronics to have replaceable batteries by 2027.

item.title: Top AI tools such as OpenClaw and Github Copilot can be hijacked to create new massive botnets
item.description: Researchers find nine of the most popular AI platforms are susceptible to a new attack that exploits hallucinations to set up a botnet.
